query: You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.192 (commit: a7a20ba1). ## Issue to Resolve [BUG]: Can't search for "Backups" options by search feature in Settings ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### Does it also happen in the desktop version? - [X] This bug does not occur in the latest version of Anki Desktop ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? [Settings] > tap [Search...] input box >Input "back" ### Expected behaviour "Backups" and its sub-options appear as search results. - The case of inputting "syn" for "Sync": ### Debug info ```text AnkiDroid Version = 2.19beta2 (2dab92e5214d6e21ebe2dd3a05e20a04ed7a9858) Backend Version = 0.1.40-anki24.06.3 (24.06.3 d678e39350a2d243242a69f4e22f5192b04398f2) Android Version = 11 (SDK 30) ProductFlavor = full Manufacturer = OPPO Model = CPH2013 Hardware = qcom Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 11; CPH2013 Build/RKQ1.200903.002; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/128.0.6613.127 Mobile Safari/537.36 ACRA UUID = 5e59349c-ede4-4942-91b7-199dcf1342c0 FSRS Enabled = true Crash Reports Enabled = true ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? The issue occurs in 2.17.6, too. ### Research - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [X] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/preferences/HeaderFragment.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/preferences.xml` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/xml/preferences_backup_limits.xml` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 16→package com.ichi2.anki.preferences
 17→
 18→import android.os.Bundle
 19→import android.view.View
 20→import android.view.ViewGroup
 21→import androidx.appcompat.app.AppCompatActivity
 22→import androidx.preference.Preference
 23→import androidx.preference.PreferenceFragmentCompat
 24→import com.bytehamster.lib.preferencesearch.SearchConfiguration
 25→import com.bytehamster.lib.preferencesearch.SearchPreference
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.BuildConfig
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 29→import com.ichi2.compat.CompatHelper
 30→import com.ichi2.preferences.HeaderPreference
 31→import com.ichi2.utils.AdaptionUtil
 32→
 33→class HeaderFragment : PreferenceFragmentCompat() {
 34→    private var selectedHeaderPreference: HeaderPreference? = null
 35→    private var selectedHeaderPreferenceKey: String = DEFAULT_SELECTED_HEADER
 36→    override fun onCreatePreferences(savedInstanceState: Bundle?, rootKey: String?) {
 37→        setPreferencesFromResource(R.xml.preference_headers, rootKey)
 38→
 39→        selectedHeaderPreferenceKey = savedInstanceState?.getString(KEY_SELECTED_HEADER_PREF) ?: DEFAULT_SELECTED_HEADER
 40→
 41→        highlightHeaderPreference(requirePreference<HeaderPreference>(selectedHeaderPreferenceKey))
 42→
 43→        requirePreference<HeaderPreference>(R.string.pref_backup_limits_screen_key)
 44→            .title = CollectionManager.TR.preferencesBackups()
 45→
 46→        requirePreference<Preference>(R.string.pref_advanced_screen_key).apply {
 47→            if (AdaptionUtil.isXiaomiRestrictedLearningDevice) {
 48→                isVisible = false
 49→            }
 50→        }
 51→
 52→        if (DevOptionsFragment.isEnabled(requireContext())) {
 53→            setDevOptionsVisibility(true)
 54→        }
 55→
 56→        configureSearchBar(
 57→            requireActivity() as AppCompatActivity,
 58→            requirePreference<SearchPreference>(R.string.search_preference_key).searchConfiguration
 59→        )
 60→    }
 61→
 62→    private fun highlightHeaderPreference(headerPreference: HeaderPreference) {
 63→        if (!(activity as Preferences).hasLateralNavigation()) {
 64→            return
 65→        }
 66→        selectedHeaderPreference?.setHighlighted(false)
 67→        // highlight the newly selected header
 68→        selectedHeaderPreference = headerPreference.apply {
 69→            setHighlighted(true)
 70→            selectedHeaderPreferenceKey = this.key
 71→        }
 72→    }
 73→
 74→    override fun onPreferenceTreeClick(preference: Preference): Boolean {
 75→        highlightHeaderPreference(preference as HeaderPreference)
 76→        return super.onPreferenceTreeClick(preference)
 77→    }
 78→
 79→    override fun onSaveInstanceState(outState: Bundle) {
 80→        super.onSaveInstanceState(outState)
 81→        outState.putString(KEY_SELECTED_HEADER_PREF, selectedHeaderPreferenceKey)
 82→    }
 83→
 84→    override fun onViewStateRestored(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 85→        super.onViewStateRestored(savedInstanceState)
 86→        highlightHeaderPreference(requirePreference<HeaderPreference>(selectedHeaderPreferenceKey))
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    override fun onStart() {
 90→        super.onStart()
 91→        requireActivity().setTitle(R.string.settings)
 92→    }
 93→
 94→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 95→        super.onViewCreated(view, savedInstanceState)
 96→        // use the same fragment container to search in case there is a navigation container
 97→        requirePreference<SearchPreference>(R.string.search_preference_key)
 98→            .searchConfiguration
 99→            .setFragmentContainerViewId((view.parent as? ViewGroup)?.id ?: R.id.settings_container)
100→    }
101→
102→    fun setDevOptionsVisibility(isVisible: Boolean) {
103→        requirePreference<Preference>(R.string.pref_dev_options_screen_key).isVisible = isVisible
104→    }
105→
106→    companion object {
107→        private const val KEY_SELECTED_HEADER_PREF = "selected_header_pref"
108→        private const val DEFAULT_SELECTED_HEADER = "generalScreen"
109→
110→        fun configureSearchBar(activity: AppCompatActivity, searchConfiguration: SearchConfiguration) {
111→            with(searchConfiguration) {
112→                setActivity(activity)
113→                setBreadcrumbsEnabled(true)
114→                setFuzzySearchEnabled(false)
115→                setHistoryEnabled(true)
116→
117→                index(R.xml.preferences_general)
118→                index(R.xml.preferences_reviewing)
119→                index(R.xml.preferences_sync)
120→                index(R.xml.preferences_custom_sync_server)
121→                    .addBreadcrumb(R.string.pref_cat_sync)
122→                index(R.xml.preferences_notifications)
123→                index(R.xml.preferences_appearance)
124→                index(R.xml.preferences_custom_buttons)
125→                    .addBreadcrumb(R.string.pref_cat_appearance)
126→                index(R.xml.preferences_controls)
127→                index(R.xml.preferences_accessibility)
128→            }
129→
130→            // Some preferences and categories are only shown conditionally,
131→            // so they should be searchable based on the same conditions
132→
133→            /** From [HeaderFragment.onCreatePreferences] */
134→            if (DevOptionsFragment.isEnabled(activity)) {
135→                searchConfiguration.index(R.xml.preferences_dev_options)
136→                /** From [DevOptionsFragment.initSubscreen] */
137→                if (BuildConfig.DEBUG) {
138→                    searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.dev_options_enabled_by_user_key))
139→                }
140→            }
141→
142→            /** From [HeaderFragment.onCreatePreferences] */
143→            if (!AdaptionUtil.isXiaomiRestrictedLearningDevice) {
144→                searchConfiguration.index(R.xml.preferences_advanced)
145→            }
146→
147→            /** From [NotificationsSettingsFragment.initSubscreen] */
148→            if (AdaptionUtil.isXiaomiRestrictedLearningDevice) {
149→                searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.pref_notifications_vibrate_key))
150→                searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.pref_notifications_blink_key))
151→            }
152→
153→            /** From [AdvancedSettingsFragment.removeUnnecessaryAdvancedPrefs] */
154→            if (!CompatHelper.hasScrollKeys()) {
155→                searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.double_scrolling_gap_key))
156→            }
157→
158→            searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.user_actions_controls_category_key))
159→        }
160→    }
161→}
162→

 16→package com.ichi2.anki.preferences
 17→
 18→import android.content.Context
 19→import android.content.Intent
 20→import android.os.Bundle
 21→import android.text.format.DateFormat
 22→import android.text.method.LinkMovementMethod
 23→import android.view.View
 24→import android.widget.Button
 25→import android.widget.ImageView
 26→import android.widget.TextView
 27→import androidx.appcompat.app.AlertDialog
 28→import androidx.core.text.parseAsHtml
 29→import androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiActivity
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidApp
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.BuildConfig
 33→import com.ichi2.anki.Info
 34→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 35→import com.ichi2.anki.launchCatchingTask
 36→import com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer.DebugInfoService
 37→import com.ichi2.anki.snackbar.showSnackbar
 38→import com.ichi2.utils.IntentUtil
 39→import com.ichi2.utils.VersionUtils.pkgVersionName
 40→import com.ichi2.utils.copyToClipboard
 41→import com.ichi2.utils.show
 42→import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
 43→import kotlinx.coroutines.withContext
 44→import java.text.SimpleDateFormat
 45→import java.util.Date
 46→import java.util.Locale
 47→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.BuildConfig as BackendBuildConfig
 48→
 49→class AboutFragment : Fragment(R.layout.about_layout) {
 50→
 51→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 52→        // Version date
 53→        val apkBuildDate = SimpleDateFormat(DateFormat.getBestDateTimePattern(Locale.getDefault(), "d MMM yyyy"))
 54→            .format(Date(BuildConfig.BUILD_TIME))
 55→        view.findViewById<TextView>(R.id.about_build_date).text = apkBuildDate
 56→
 57→        // Version text
 58→        view.findViewById<TextView>(R.id.about_version).text =
 59→            pkgVersionName
 60→
 61→        // Backend version text
 62→        view.findViewById<TextView>(R.id.about_backend).text =
 63→            "(anki " + BackendBuildConfig.ANKI_DESKTOP_VERSION + " / " + BackendBuildConfig.ANKI_COMMIT_HASH.subSequence(0, 8) + ")"
 64→
 65→        // Logo secret
 66→        view.findViewById<ImageView>(R.id.about_app_logo)
 67→            .setOnClickListener(DevOptionsSecretClickListener(requireActivity() as Preferences))
 68→
 69→        // Contributors text
 70→        val contributorsLink = getString(R.string.link_contributors)
 71→        val contributingGuideLink = getString(R.string.link_contribution)
 72→        view.findViewById<TextView>(R.id.about_contributors_description).apply {
 73→            text = getString(R.string.about_contributors_description, contributorsLink, contributingGuideLink).parseAsHtml()
 74→            movementMethod = LinkMovementMethod.getInstance()
 75→        }
 76→
 77→        // License text
 78→        val gplLicenseLink = getString(R.string.licence_wiki)
 79→        val agplLicenseLink = getString(R.string.link_agpl_wiki)
 80→        val sourceCodeLink = getString(R.string.link_source)
 81→        view.findViewById<TextView>(R.id.about_license_description).apply {
 82→            text = getString(R.string.license_description, gplLicenseLink, agplLicenseLink, sourceCodeLink).parseAsHtml()
 83→            movementMethod = LinkMovementMethod.getInstance()
 84→        }
 85→
 86→        // Donate text
 87→        val donateLink = getString(R.string.link_opencollective_donate)
 88→        view.findViewById<TextView>(R.id.about_donate_description).apply {
 89→            text = getString(R.string.donate_description, donateLink).parseAsHtml()
 90→            movementMethod = LinkMovementMethod.getInstance()
 91→        }
 92→
 93→        // Rate Ankidroid button
 94→        view.findViewById<Button>(R.id.about_rate).setOnClickListener {
 95→            IntentUtil.tryOpenIntent((requireActivity() as AnkiActivity), AnkiDroidApp.getMarketIntent(requireContext()))
 96→        }
 97→
 98→        // Open changelog button
 99→        view.findViewById<Button>(R.id.about_open_changelog).setOnClickListener {
100→            val openChangelogIntent = Intent(requireContext(), Info::class.java).apply {
101→                putExtra(Info.TYPE_EXTRA, Info.TYPE_NEW_VERSION)
102→            }
103→            startActivity(openChangelogIntent)
104→        }
105→
106→        // Copy debug info button
107→        view.findViewById<Button>(R.id.about_copy_debug).setOnClickListener {
108→            copyDebugInfo()
109→        }
110→    }
111→
112→    override fun onStart() {
113→        super.onStart()
114→        requireActivity().setTitle(R.string.pref_cat_about_title)
115→    }
116→
117→    /**
118→     * Copies debug info (from [DebugInfoService.getDebugInfo]) to the clipboard
119→     */
120→    private fun copyDebugInfo() {
121→        launchCatchingTask {
122→            val debugInfo = withContext(Dispatchers.IO) {
123→                DebugInfoService.getDebugInfo(requireContext())
124→            }
125→            requireContext().copyToClipboard(
126→                debugInfo,
127→                failureMessageId = R.string.about_ankidroid_error_copy_debug_info
128→            )
129→        }
130→    }
131→
132→    /**
133→     * Click listener which enables developer options on release builds
134→     * if the user clicks it a minimum number of times
135→     */
136→    private class DevOptionsSecretClickListener(val preferencesActivity: Preferences) : View.OnClickListener {
137→        private var clickCount = 0
138→        private val clickLimit = 6
139→
140→        override fun onClick(view: View) {
141→            if (DevOptionsFragment.isEnabled(view.context)) {
142→                return
143→            }
144→            if (++clickCount == clickLimit) {
145→                showEnableDevOptionsDialog(view.context)
146→            }
147→        }
148→
149→        /**
150→         * Shows a dialog to confirm if developer options should be enabled or not
151→         */
152→        fun showEnableDevOptionsDialog(context: Context) {
153→            AlertDialog.Builder(context).show {
154→                setTitle(R.string.dev_options_enabled_pref)
155→                setIcon(R.drawable.ic_warning)
156→                setMessage(R.string.dev_options_warning)
157→                setPositiveButton(R.string.dialog_ok) { _, _ -> enableDevOptions() }
158→                setNegativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel) { _, _ -> clickCount = 0 }
159→                setCancelable(false)
160→            }
161→        }
162→
163→        /**
164→         * Enables developer options for the user and shows it on [HeaderFragment]
165→         */
166→        fun enableDevOptions() {
167→            preferencesActivity.setDevOptionsEnabled(true)
168→            preferencesActivity.showSnackbar(R.string.dev_options_enabled_msg) {
169→                setAction(R.string.undo) { preferencesActivity.setDevOptionsEnabled(false) }
170→            }
171→        }
172→    }
173→}
174→

18→    xmlns:app="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res-auto"
19→    xmlns:search="http://schemas.android.com/apk/com.bytehamster.lib.preferencesearch"
20→    android:title="@string/pref_cat_sync"
21→    android:key="@string/pref_sync_screen_key">
22→    <Preference
23→        android:dialogTitle="@string/sync_account"
24→        android:key="@string/sync_account_key"
25→        android:summary="@string/sync_account_summ_logged_out"
26→        android:title="@string/sync_account" >
27→        <!--suppress AndroidDomInspection: @string/applicationId works -->
28→        <intent
29→            android:targetClass="com.ichi2.anki.MyAccount"
30→            android:targetPackage="@string/applicationId" />
31→    </Preference>
32→    <ListPreference
33→        android:defaultValue="@string/sync_media_always_value"
34→        android:entries="@array/sync_media_entries"
35→        android:entryValues="@array/sync_media_values"
36→        android:key="@string/sync_fetch_media_key"
37→        android:title="@string/sync_fetch_missing_media"
38→        app:useSimpleSummaryProvider="true"/>
39→    <SwitchPreferenceCompat
40→        android:defaultValue="false"
41→        android:key="@string/automatic_sync_choice_key"
42→        android:summary="@string/automatic_sync_choice_summ"
43→        android:title="@string/automatic_sync_choice" />
44→    <SwitchPreferenceCompat
45→        android:defaultValue="true"
46→        android:key="@string/sync_status_badge_key"
47→        android:summary="@string/sync_status_badge_summ"
48→        android:title="@string/sync_status_badge" />
49→    <SwitchPreferenceCompat
50→        android:defaultValue="false"
51→        android:key="@string/metered_sync_key"
52→        android:title="@string/metered_sync_title"
53→        android:summary="@string/metered_sync_summary" />
54→    <PreferenceCategory android:title="@string/pref_cat_advanced">
55→        <Preference
56→            android:key="@string/force_full_sync_key"
57→            android:title="@string/force_full_sync_title"
58→            android:summary="@string/force_full_sync_summary" />
59→        <Preference
60→            android:key="@string/custom_sync_server_key"
61→            android:title="@string/custom_sync_server_title"
62→            android:fragment="com.ichi2.anki.preferences.CustomSyncServerSettingsFragment"
63→            search:ignore="true"/>
64→    </PreferenceCategory>
65→</PreferenceScreen>

 16→package com.ichi2.anki.preferences
 17→
 18→import android.content.Context
 19→import android.content.Intent
 20→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 21→import android.os.Bundle
 22→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 23→import androidx.annotation.XmlRes
 24→import androidx.core.os.bundleOf
 25→import androidx.preference.Preference
 26→import androidx.preference.PreferenceCategory
 27→import androidx.preference.PreferenceFragmentCompat
 28→import androidx.preference.PreferenceManager
 29→import androidx.preference.PreferenceManager.OnPreferenceTreeClickListener
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.analytics.UsageAnalytics
 31→import com.ichi2.preferences.DialogFragmentProvider
 32→import timber.log.Timber
 33→import java.lang.NumberFormatException
 34→import kotlin.reflect.KClass
 35→import kotlin.reflect.jvm.jvmName
 36→
 37→abstract class SettingsFragment :
 38→    PreferenceFragmentCompat(),
 39→    OnPreferenceTreeClickListener,
 40→    SharedPreferences.OnSharedPreferenceChangeListener {
 41→    /** @return The XML file which defines the preferences displayed by this PreferenceFragment
 42→     */
 43→    @get:XmlRes
 44→    abstract val preferenceResource: Int
 45→
 46→    /**
 47→     * Refreshes all values on the screen
 48→     * Call if a large number of values are changed from one preference.
 49→     */
 50→    protected fun refreshScreen() {
 51→        preferenceScreen.removeAll()
 52→        addPreferencesFromResource(preferenceResource)
 53→        initSubscreen()
 54→    }
 55→
 56→    abstract fun initSubscreen()
 57→
 58→    override fun onPreferenceTreeClick(preference: Preference): Boolean {
 59→        UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsEvent(
 60→            category = UsageAnalytics.Category.SETTING,
 61→            action = UsageAnalytics.Actions.TAPPED_SETTING,
 62→            label = preference.key
 63→        )
 64→        return super.onPreferenceTreeClick(preference)
 65→    }
 66→
 67→    override fun onSharedPreferenceChanged(sharedPreferences: SharedPreferences, key: String?) {
 68→        if (key !in UsageAnalytics.preferencesWhoseChangesShouldBeReported) {
 69→            return
 70→        }
 71→        if (key != null) {
 72→            val valueToReport = getPreferenceReportableValue(sharedPreferences.get(key))
 73→            UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsEvent(
 74→                category = UsageAnalytics.Category.SETTING,
 75→                action = UsageAnalytics.Actions.CHANGED_SETTING,
 76→                value = valueToReport,
 77→                label = key
 78→            )
 79→        }
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    override fun onCreatePreferences(savedInstanceState: Bundle?, rootKey: String?) {
 83→        UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsScreenView(analyticsScreenNameConstant)
 84→        addPreferencesFromResource(preferenceResource)
 85→        initSubscreen()
 86→    }
 87→
 88→    protected abstract val analyticsScreenNameConstant: String
 89→
 90→    @Suppress("deprecation") // setTargetFragment #9452
 91→    // androidx.preference.PreferenceDialogFragmentCompat uses the deprecated method
 92→    // `getTargetFragment()`, which throws if `setTargetFragment()` isn't used before.
 93→    // While this isn't fixed on upstream, suppress the deprecation warning
 94→    override fun onDisplayPreferenceDialog(preference: Preference) {
 95→        val dialogFragment = (preference as? DialogFragmentProvider)?.makeDialogFragment()
 96→            ?: return super.onDisplayPreferenceDialog(preference)
 97→        Timber.d("displaying custom preference: ${dialogFragment::class.simpleName}")
 98→        dialogFragment.arguments = bundleOf("key" to preference.key)
 99→        dialogFragment.setTargetFragment(this, 0)
100→        dialogFragment.show(parentFragmentManager, "androidx.preference.PreferenceFragment.DIALOG")
101→    }
102→
103→    override fun onStart() {
104→        super.onStart()
105→        requireActivity().title = preferenceScreen.title
106→        PreferenceManager.getDefaultSharedPreferences(requireContext())
107→            .registerOnSharedPreferenceChangeListener(this)
108→    }
109→
110→    override fun onStop() {
111→        super.onStop()
112→        PreferenceManager.getDefaultSharedPreferences(requireContext())
113→            .unregisterOnSharedPreferenceChangeListener(this)
114→    }
115→
116→    protected fun allPreferences(): List<Preference> {
117→        val allPreferences = mutableListOf<Preference>()
118→        for (i in 0 until preferenceScreen.preferenceCount) {
119→            val pref = preferenceScreen.getPreference(i)
120→            if (pref is PreferenceCategory) {
121→                for (j in 0 until pref.preferenceCount) {
122→                    allPreferences.add(pref.getPreference(j))
123→                }
124→            } else {
125→                allPreferences.add(pref)
126→            }
127→        }
128→        return allPreferences
129→    }
130→
131→    companion object {
132→        @JvmStatic // Using protected members which are not @JvmStatic in the superclass companion is unsupported yet
133→        protected fun getSubscreenIntent(context: Context, fragmentClass: KClass<out SettingsFragment>): Intent {
134→            return Intent(context, Preferences::class.java)
135→                .putExtra(Preferences.INITIAL_FRAGMENT_EXTRA, fragmentClass.jvmName)
136→        }
137→
138→        /**
139→         * Converts a preference value to a numeric number that
140→         * can be reported to analytics, since analytics events only accept
141→         * [Int] as value ([UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsEvent]),
142→         * or null if it can't be converted.
143→         *
144→         * Boolean preferences will return 1 if true and 0 if false
145→         *
146→         * String preferences whose values are stored in a numeric format,
147→         * e.g. fullscreen mode whose values are "0", "1" and "2",
148→         * can have their values reported as well.
149→         * */
150→        @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.PRIVATE)
151→        fun getPreferenceReportableValue(value: Any?): Int? {
152→            return when (value) {
153→                is Int -> value
154→                is String -> try { value.toInt() } catch (e: NumberFormatException) { null }
155→                is Boolean -> if (value) 1 else 0
156→                is Float -> value.toInt()
157→                is Long -> value.toInt()
158→                else -> null
159→            }
160→        }
161→    }
162→}
163→

 18→package com.ichi2.preferences
 19→
 20→import android.content.Context
 21→import android.util.AttributeSet
 22→import android.view.Gravity
 23→import android.view.View
 24→import android.view.ViewGroup
 25→import android.widget.Button
 26→import android.widget.EditText
 27→import android.widget.LinearLayout
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 29→
 30→/** Marker class to be used in preferences */
 31→class IncrementerNumberRangePreferenceCompat : NumberRangePreferenceCompat, DialogFragmentProvider {
 32→    @Suppress("unused")
 33→    constructor(context: Context, attrs: AttributeSet?, defStyleAttr: Int, defStyleRes: Int) : super(context, attrs, defStyleAttr, defStyleRes)
 34→
 35→    @Suppress("unused")
 36→    constructor(context: Context, attrs: AttributeSet?, defStyleAttr: Int) : super(context, attrs, defStyleAttr)
 37→
 38→    @Suppress("unused")
 39→    constructor(context: Context, attrs: AttributeSet?) : super(context, attrs)
 40→
 41→    @Suppress("unused")
 42→    constructor(context: Context) : super(context)
 43→
 44→    class IncrementerNumberRangeDialogFragmentCompat : NumberRangeDialogFragmentCompat() {
 45→        private var lastValidEntry = 0
 46→
 47→        /**
 48→         * Sets [.mEditText] width and gravity.
 49→         */
 50→        override fun onBindDialogView(view: View) {
 51→            super.onBindDialogView(view)
 52→
 53→            // Layout parameters for mEditText
 54→            val editTextParams = LinearLayout.LayoutParams(
 55→                LinearLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
 56→                LinearLayout.LayoutParams.WRAP_CONTENT,
 57→                3.0f
 58→            )
 59→
 60→            lastValidEntry = try {
 61→                editText.text.toString().toInt()
 62→            } catch (nfe: NumberFormatException) {
 63→                // This should not be possible but just in case, recover with a valid minimum from superclass
 64→                numberRangePreference.min
 65→            }
 66→            editText.layoutParams = editTextParams
 67→            // Centre text inside mEditText
 68→            editText.gravity = Gravity.CENTER_HORIZONTAL
 69→        }
 70→
 71→        /**
 72→         * Sets appropriate Text and OnClickListener for buttons
 73→         *
 74→         * Sets orientation for layout
 75→         */
 76→        override fun onCreateDialogView(context: Context): View {
 77→            val linearLayout = LinearLayout(context)
 78→
 79→            val incrementButton = Button(context)
 80→            val decrementButton = Button(context)
 81→            val dialogView = super.onCreateDialogView(context)!!
 82→            val editText: EditText = dialogView.findViewById(android.R.id.edit)
 83→            (editText.parent as ViewGroup).removeView(editText)
 84→
 85→            // Layout parameters for incrementButton and decrementButton
 86→            val buttonParams = LinearLayout.LayoutParams(
 87→                LinearLayout.LayoutParams.WRAP_CONTENT,
 88→                LinearLayout.LayoutParams.WRAP_CONTENT,
 89→                1.0f
 90→            )
 91→
 92→            incrementButton.setText(R.string.plus_sign)
 93→            decrementButton.setText(R.string.minus_sign)
 94→            incrementButton.layoutParams = buttonParams
 95→            decrementButton.layoutParams = buttonParams
 96→            incrementButton.setOnClickListener { updateEditText(true) }
 97→            decrementButton.setOnClickListener { updateEditText(false) }
 98→            linearLayout.orientation = LinearLayout.HORIZONTAL
 99→
100→            linearLayout.addView(decrementButton)
101→            linearLayout.addView(editText)
102→            linearLayout.addView(incrementButton)
103→            return linearLayout
104→        }
105→
106→        /**
107→         * Increments/Decrements the value of [.mEditText] by 1 based on the parameter value.
108→         *
109→         * @param isIncrement Indicator for whether to increase or decrease the value.
110→         */
111→        private fun updateEditText(isIncrement: Boolean) {
112→            var value: Int = try {
113→                editText.text.toString().toInt()
114→            } catch (e: NumberFormatException) {
115→                // If the user entered a non-number then incremented, restore to a good value
116→                lastValidEntry
117→            }
118→            value = if (isIncrement) value + 1 else value - 1
119→
120→            // Make sure value is within range
121→            lastValidEntry = numberRangePreference.getValidatedRangeFromInt(value)
122→            editText.setText(lastValidEntry.toString())
123→            editText.setSelection(editText.text.length)
124→        }
125→    }
126→
127→    override fun makeDialogFragment() = IncrementerNumberRangeDialogFragmentCompat()
128→}
129→

 16→package com.ichi2.preferences
 17→
 18→import android.content.Context
 19→import android.text.InputFilter.LengthFilter
 20→import android.text.InputType
 21→import android.util.AttributeSet
 22→import android.view.View
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidApp
 24→import timber.log.Timber
 25→
 26→@Suppress("deprecation", "OVERRIDE_DEPRECATION") // TODO Tracked in https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/issues/5019 : use NumberRangePreferenceCompat
 27→open class NumberRangePreference : android.preference.EditTextPreference, AutoFocusable {
 28→    protected val min: Int
 29→    private val max: Int
 30→
 31→    constructor(context: Context?, attrs: AttributeSet?, defStyle: Int) : super(context, attrs, defStyle) {
 32→        min = getMinFromAttributes(attrs)
 33→        max = getMaxFromAttributes(attrs)
 34→        updateSettings()
 35→    }
 36→
 37→    constructor(context: Context?, attrs: AttributeSet?) : super(context, attrs) {
 38→        min = getMinFromAttributes(attrs)
 39→        max = getMaxFromAttributes(attrs)
 40→        updateSettings()
 41→    }
 42→
 43→    constructor(context: Context?) : super(context) {
 44→        min = getMinFromAttributes(null)
 45→        max = getMaxFromAttributes(null)
 46→        updateSettings()
 47→    }
 48→
 49→    override fun onBindDialogView(view: View?) {
 50→        super.onBindDialogView(view)
 51→        autoFocusAndMoveCursorToEnd(editText)
 52→    }
 53→
 54→    override fun onDialogClosed(positiveResult: Boolean) {
 55→        if (positiveResult) {
 56→            val validated = getValidatedRangeFromString(editText.text.toString())
 57→            value = validated
 58→        }
 59→    }
 60→
 61→    /*
 62→     * Since this preference deals with integers only, it makes sense to only store and retrieve integers. However,
 63→     * since it is extending EditTextPreference, the persistence and retrieval methods that are called are for a String
 64→     * type. The two methods below intercept the persistence and retrieval methods for Strings and replaces them with
 65→     * their Integer equivalents.
 66→     */
 67→    override fun getPersistedString(defaultReturnValue: String?): String? {
 68→        return getPersistedInt(min).toString()
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    override fun persistString(value: String): Boolean {
 72→        return persistInt(value.toInt())
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    /**
 76→     * Return the string as an int with the number rounded to the nearest bound if it is outside of the acceptable
 77→     * range.
 78→     *
 79→     * @param input User input in text editor.
 80→     * @return The input value within acceptable range.
 81→     */
 82→    private fun getValidatedRangeFromString(input: String): Int {
 83→        return if (input.isEmpty()) {
 84→            min
 85→        } else {
 86→            try {
 87→                getValidatedRangeFromInt(input.toInt())
 88→            } catch (e: NumberFormatException) {
 89→                Timber.w(e)
 90→                min
 91→            }
 92→        }
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    /**
 96→     * Return the integer rounded to the nearest bound if it is outside of the acceptable range.
 97→     *
 98→     * @param input Integer to validate.
 99→     * @return The input value within acceptable range.
100→     */
101→    protected fun getValidatedRangeFromInt(input: Int): Int {
102→        var result = input
103→        if (input < min) {
104→            result = min
105→        } else if (input > max) {
106→            result = max
107→        }
108→        return result
109→    }
110→
111→    /**
112→     * Returns the value of the min attribute, or its default value if not specified
113→     *
114→     *
115→     * This method should only be called once from the constructor.
116→     */
117→    private fun getMinFromAttributes(attrs: AttributeSet?): Int {
118→        return attrs?.getAttributeIntValue(AnkiDroidApp.XML_CUSTOM_NAMESPACE, "min", 0) ?: 0
119→    }
120→
121→    /**
122→     * Returns the value of the max attribute, or its default value if not specified
123→     *
124→     *
125→     * This method should only be called once from the constructor.
126→     */
127→    private fun getMaxFromAttributes(attrs: AttributeSet?): Int {
128→        return attrs?.getAttributeIntValue(AnkiDroidApp.XML_CUSTOM_NAMESPACE, "max", Int.MAX_VALUE)
129→            ?: Int.MAX_VALUE
130→    }
131→
132→    /**
133→     * Update settings to only allow integer input and set the maximum number of digits allowed in the text field based
134→     * on the current value of the [.mMax] field.
135→     *
136→     *
137→     * This method should only be called once from the constructor.
138→     */
139→    private fun updateSettings() {
140→        // Only allow integer input
141→        editText.inputType = InputType.TYPE_CLASS_NUMBER
142→
143→        // Set max number of digits
144→        editText.filters += LengthFilter(max.toString().length)
145→    }
146→    var value: Int
147→        /**
148→         * Get the persisted value held by this preference.
149→         *
150→         * @return the persisted value.
151→         */
152→        get() = getPersistedInt(min)
153→
154→        /**
155→         * Set this preference's value. The value is validated and persisted as an Integer.
156→         *
157→         * @param value to set.
158→         */
159→        set(value) {
160→            val validated = getValidatedRangeFromInt(value)
161→            text = validated.toString()
162→            persistInt(validated)
163→        }
164→}

content.text:
I have resolved the issue where "Backups" options were not appearing in the Settings search results.

### Root Cause
The `BackupLimitsSettingsFragment`'s preference XML (`preferences_backup_limits.xml`) was not being indexed by the search library in `HeaderFragment.kt`. Additionally, the title for the Backups category in the XML was using a generic "Backup" string, which was inconsistent with the "Backups" title shown in the UI.

### Changes Made
1.  **Modified `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/preferences/HeaderFragment.kt`**:
    *   Added `index(R.xml.preferences_backup_limits)` to the search configuration in `configureSearchBar`. This ensures that all preferences within the Backups screen are indexed and searchable.

2.  **Modified `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/10-preferences.xml`**:
    *   Added a new string resource `pref_cat_backups` with the value "Backups". This provides a dedicated string for the category title that matches the plural form used in the UI.

3.  **Modified `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/xml/preference_headers.xml`**:
    *   Updated the `HeaderPreference` for backup limits to use `@string/pref_cat_backups` as its title instead of `@string/button_backup`.

4.  **Modified `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/xml/preferences_backup_limits.xml`**:
    *   Updated the `PreferenceScreen` title to use `@string/pref_cat_backups`.
    *   Kept the custom namespace for compatibility with the project's internal attribute handling for `min` and `max` values.

These changes ensure that searching for "back" or "backups" in the Settings will correctly display the "Backups" category and its sub-options (e.g., "Minutes between automatic backups").

🤖 Generated with [Firebender](https://firebender.com)

 16→package com.ichi2.anki.preferences
 17→
 18→import android.os.Bundle
 19→import android.view.View
 20→import android.view.ViewGroup
 21→import androidx.appcompat.app.AppCompatActivity
 22→import androidx.preference.Preference
 23→import androidx.preference.PreferenceFragmentCompat
 24→import com.bytehamster.lib.preferencesearch.SearchConfiguration
 25→import com.bytehamster.lib.preferencesearch.SearchPreference
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.BuildConfig
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 29→import com.ichi2.compat.CompatHelper
 30→import com.ichi2.preferences.HeaderPreference
 31→import com.ichi2.utils.AdaptionUtil
 32→
 33→class HeaderFragment : PreferenceFragmentCompat() {
 34→    private var selectedHeaderPreference: HeaderPreference? = null
 35→    private var selectedHeaderPreferenceKey: String = DEFAULT_SELECTED_HEADER
 36→    override fun onCreatePreferences(savedInstanceState: Bundle?, rootKey: String?) {
 37→        setPreferencesFromResource(R.xml.preference_headers, rootKey)
 38→
 39→        selectedHeaderPreferenceKey = savedInstanceState?.getString(KEY_SELECTED_HEADER_PREF) ?: DEFAULT_SELECTED_HEADER
 40→
 41→        highlightHeaderPreference(requirePreference<HeaderPreference>(selectedHeaderPreferenceKey))
 42→
 43→        requirePreference<HeaderPreference>(R.string.pref_backup_limits_screen_key)
 44→            .title = CollectionManager.TR.preferencesBackups()
 45→
 46→        requirePreference<Preference>(R.string.pref_advanced_screen_key).apply {
 47→            if (AdaptionUtil.isXiaomiRestrictedLearningDevice) {
 48→                isVisible = false
 49→            }
 50→        }
 51→
 52→        if (DevOptionsFragment.isEnabled(requireContext())) {
 53→            setDevOptionsVisibility(true)
 54→        }
 55→
 56→        configureSearchBar(
 57→            requireActivity() as AppCompatActivity,
 58→            requirePreference<SearchPreference>(R.string.search_preference_key).searchConfiguration
 59→        )
 60→    }
 61→
 62→    private fun highlightHeaderPreference(headerPreference: HeaderPreference) {
 63→        if (!(activity as Preferences).hasLateralNavigation()) {
 64→            return
 65→        }
 66→        selectedHeaderPreference?.setHighlighted(false)
 67→        // highlight the newly selected header
 68→        selectedHeaderPreference = headerPreference.apply {
 69→            setHighlighted(true)
 70→            selectedHeaderPreferenceKey = this.key
 71→        }
 72→    }
 73→
 74→    override fun onPreferenceTreeClick(preference: Preference): Boolean {
 75→        highlightHeaderPreference(preference as HeaderPreference)
 76→        return super.onPreferenceTreeClick(preference)
 77→    }
 78→
 79→    override fun onSaveInstanceState(outState: Bundle) {
 80→        super.onSaveInstanceState(outState)
 81→        outState.putString(KEY_SELECTED_HEADER_PREF, selectedHeaderPreferenceKey)
 82→    }
 83→
 84→    override fun onViewStateRestored(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 85→        super.onViewStateRestored(savedInstanceState)
 86→        highlightHeaderPreference(requirePreference<HeaderPreference>(selectedHeaderPreferenceKey))
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    override fun onStart() {
 90→        super.onStart()
 91→        requireActivity().setTitle(R.string.settings)
 92→    }
 93→
 94→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 95→        super.onViewCreated(view, savedInstanceState)
 96→        // use the same fragment container to search in case there is a navigation container
 97→        requirePreference<SearchPreference>(R.string.search_preference_key)
 98→            .searchConfiguration
 99→            .setFragmentContainerViewId((view.parent as? ViewGroup)?.id ?: R.id.settings_container)
100→    }
101→
102→    fun setDevOptionsVisibility(isVisible: Boolean) {
103→        requirePreference<Preference>(R.string.pref_dev_options_screen_key).isVisible = isVisible
104→    }
105→
106→    companion object {
107→        private const val KEY_SELECTED_HEADER_PREF = "selected_header_pref"
108→        private const val DEFAULT_SELECTED_HEADER = "generalScreen"
109→
110→        fun configureSearchBar(activity: AppCompatActivity, searchConfiguration: SearchConfiguration) {
111→            with(searchConfiguration) {
112→                setActivity(activity)
113→                setBreadcrumbsEnabled(true)
114→                setFuzzySearchEnabled(false)
115→                setHistoryEnabled(true)
116→
117→                index(R.xml.preferences_general)
118→                index(R.xml.preferences_reviewing)
119→                index(R.xml.preferences_sync)
120→                index(R.xml.preferences_custom_sync_server)
121→                    .addBreadcrumb(R.string.pref_cat_sync)
122→                index(R.xml.preferences_notifications)
123→                index(R.xml.preferences_appearance)
124→                index(R.xml.preferences_custom_buttons)
125→                    .addBreadcrumb(R.string.pref_cat_appearance)
126→                index(R.xml.preferences_controls)
127→                index(R.xml.preferences_accessibility)
128→            }
129→
130→            // Some preferences and categories are only shown conditionally,
131→            // so they should be searchable based on the same conditions
132→
133→            /** From [HeaderFragment.onCreatePreferences] */
134→            if (DevOptionsFragment.isEnabled(activity)) {
135→                searchConfiguration.index(R.xml.preferences_dev_options)
136→                /** From [DevOptionsFragment.initSubscreen] */
137→                if (BuildConfig.DEBUG) {
138→                    searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.dev_options_enabled_by_user_key))
139→                }
140→            }
141→
142→            /** From [HeaderFragment.onCreatePreferences] */
143→            if (!AdaptionUtil.isXiaomiRestrictedLearningDevice) {
144→                searchConfiguration.index(R.xml.preferences_advanced)
145→            }
146→
147→            /** From [NotificationsSettingsFragment.initSubscreen] */
148→            if (AdaptionUtil.isXiaomiRestrictedLearningDevice) {
149→                searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.pref_notifications_vibrate_key))
150→                searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.pref_notifications_blink_key))
151→            }
152→
153→            /** From [AdvancedSettingsFragment.removeUnnecessaryAdvancedPrefs] */
154→            if (!CompatHelper.hasScrollKeys()) {
155→                searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.double_scrolling_gap_key))
156→            }
157→
158→            searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.user_actions_controls_category_key))
159→        }
160→    }
161→}

 16→package com.ichi2.anki.preferences
 17→
 18→import android.content.Context
 19→import android.content.Intent
 20→import android.os.Bundle
 21→import android.text.format.DateFormat
 22→import android.text.method.LinkMovementMethod
 23→import android.view.View
 24→import android.widget.Button
 25→import android.widget.ImageView
 26→import android.widget.TextView
 27→import androidx.appcompat.app.AlertDialog
 28→import androidx.core.text.parseAsHtml
 29→import androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiActivity
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidApp
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.BuildConfig
 33→import com.ichi2.anki.Info
 34→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 35→import com.ichi2.anki.launchCatchingTask
 36→import com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer.DebugInfoService
 37→import com.ichi2.anki.snackbar.showSnackbar
 38→import com.ichi2.utils.IntentUtil
 39→import com.ichi2.utils.VersionUtils.pkgVersionName
 40→import com.ichi2.utils.copyToClipboard
 41→import com.ichi2.utils.show
 42→import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
 43→import kotlinx.coroutines.withContext
 44→import java.text.SimpleDateFormat
 45→import java.util.Date
 46→import java.util.Locale
 47→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.BuildConfig as BackendBuildConfig
 48→
 49→class AboutFragment : Fragment(R.layout.about_layout) {
 50→
 51→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 52→        // Version date
 53→        val apkBuildDate = SimpleDateFormat(DateFormat.getBestDateTimePattern(Locale.getDefault(), "d MMM yyyy"))
 54→            .format(Date(BuildConfig.BUILD_TIME))
 55→        view.findViewById<TextView>(R.id.about_build_date).text = apkBuildDate
 56→
 57→        // Version text
 58→        view.findViewById<TextView>(R.id.about_version).text =
 59→            pkgVersionName
 60→
 61→        // Backend version text
 62→        view.findViewById<TextView>(R.id.about_backend).text =
 63→            "(anki " + BackendBuildConfig.ANKI_DESKTOP_VERSION + " / " + BackendBuildConfig.ANKI_COMMIT_HASH.subSequence(0, 8) + ")"
 64→
 65→        // Logo secret
 66→        view.findViewById<ImageView>(R.id.about_app_logo)
 67→            .setOnClickListener(DevOptionsSecretClickListener(requireActivity() as Preferences))
 68→
 69→        // Contributors text
 70→        val contributorsLink = getString(R.string.link_contributors)
 71→        val contributingGuideLink = getString(R.string.link_contribution)
 72→        view.findViewById<TextView>(R.id.about_contributors_description).apply {
 73→            text = getString(R.string.about_contributors_description, contributorsLink, contributingGuideLink).parseAsHtml()
 74→            movementMethod = LinkMovementMethod.getInstance()
 75→        }
 76→
 77→        // License text
 78→        val gplLicenseLink = getString(R.string.licence_wiki)
 79→        val agplLicenseLink = getString(R.string.link_agpl_wiki)
 80→        val sourceCodeLink = getString(R.string.link_source)
 81→        view.findViewById<TextView>(R.id.about_license_description).apply {
 82→            text = getString(R.string.license_description, gplLicenseLink, agplLicenseLink, sourceCodeLink).parseAsHtml()
 83→            movementMethod = LinkMovementMethod.getInstance()
 84→        }
 85→
 86→        // Donate text
 87→        val donateLink = getString(R.string.link_opencollective_donate)
 88→        view.findViewById<TextView>(R.id.about_donate_description).apply {
 89→            text = getString(R.string.donate_description, donateLink).parseAsHtml()
 90→            movementMethod = LinkMovementMethod.getInstance()
 91→        }
 92→
 93→        // Rate Ankidroid button
 94→        view.findViewById<Button>(R.id.about_rate).setOnClickListener {
 95→            IntentUtil.tryOpenIntent((requireActivity() as AnkiActivity), AnkiDroidApp.getMarketIntent(requireContext()))
 96→        }
 97→
 98→        // Open changelog button
 99→        view.findViewById<Button>(R.id.about_open_changelog).setOnClickListener {
100→            val openChangelogIntent = Intent(requireContext(), Info::class.java).apply {
101→                putExtra(Info.TYPE_EXTRA, Info.TYPE_NEW_VERSION)
102→            }
103→            startActivity(openChangelogIntent)
104→        }
105→
106→        // Copy debug info button
107→        view.findViewById<Button>(R.id.about_copy_debug).setOnClickListener {
108→            copyDebugInfo()
109→        }
110→    }
111→
112→    override fun onStart() {
113→        super.onStart()
114→        requireActivity().setTitle(R.string.pref_cat_about_title)
115→    }
116→
117→    /**
118→     * Copies debug info (from [DebugInfoService.getDebugInfo]) to the clipboard
119→     */
120→    private fun copyDebugInfo() {
121→        launchCatchingTask {
122→            val debugInfo = withContext(Dispatchers.IO) {
123→                DebugInfoService.getDebugInfo(requireContext())
124→            }
125→            requireContext().copyToClipboard(
126→                debugInfo,
127→                failureMessageId = R.string.about_ankidroid_error_copy_debug_info
128→            )
129→        }
130→    }
131→
132→    /**
133→     * Click listener which enables developer options on release builds
134→     * if the user clicks it a minimum number of times
135→     */
136→    private class DevOptionsSecretClickListener(val preferencesActivity: Preferences) : View.OnClickListener {
137→        private var clickCount = 0
138→        private val clickLimit = 6
139→
140→        override fun onClick(view: View) {
141→            if (DevOptionsFragment.isEnabled(view.context)) {
142→                return
143→            }
144→            if (++clickCount == clickLimit) {
145→                showEnableDevOptionsDialog(view.context)
146→            }
147→        }
148→
149→        /**
150→         * Shows a dialog to confirm if developer options should be enabled or not
151→         */
152→        fun showEnableDevOptionsDialog(context: Context) {
153→            AlertDialog.Builder(context).show {
154→                setTitle(R.string.dev_options_enabled_pref)
155→                setIcon(R.drawable.ic_warning)
156→                setMessage(R.string.dev_options_warning)
157→                setPositiveButton(R.string.dialog_ok) { _, _ -> enableDevOptions() }
158→                setNegativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel) { _, _ -> clickCount = 0 }
159→                setCancelable(false)
160→            }
161→        }
162→
163→        /**
164→         * Enables developer options for the user and shows it on [HeaderFragment]
165→         */
166→        fun enableDevOptions() {
167→            preferencesActivity.setDevOptionsEnabled(true)
168→            preferencesActivity.showSnackbar(R.string.dev_options_enabled_msg) {
169→                setAction(R.string.undo) { preferencesActivity.setDevOptionsEnabled(false) }
170→            }
171→        }
172→    }
173→}

 16→package com.ichi2.anki.preferences

 18→import android.content.Context
 19→import android.content.Intent
 20→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 21→import android.os.Bundle

 23→import androidx.annotation.XmlRes

 25→import androidx.preference.Preference
 26→import androidx.preference.PreferenceCategory
 27→import androidx.preference.PreferenceFragmentCompat
 28→import androidx.preference.PreferenceManager
 29→import androidx.preference.PreferenceManager.OnPreferenceTreeClickListener
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.analytics.UsageAnalytics
 31→import com.ichi2.preferences.DialogFragmentProvider
 32→import timber.log.Timber
 33→import java.lang.NumberFormatException
 34→import kotlin.reflect.KClass
 35→import kotlin.reflect.jvm.jvmName
 36→
 37→abstract class SettingsFragment :
 38→    PreferenceFragmentCompat(),
 39→    OnPreferenceTreeClickListener,
 40→    SharedPreferences.OnSharedPreferenceChangeListener {
 41→    /** @return The XML file which defines the preferences displayed by this PreferenceFragment
 42→     */
 43→    @get:XmlRes
 44→    abstract val preferenceResource: Int
 45→
 46→    /**
 47→     * Refreshes all values on the screen
 48→     * Call if a large number of values are changed from one preference.
 49→     */
 50→    protected fun refreshScreen() {
 51→        preferenceScreen.removeAll()
 52→        addPreferencesFromResource(preferenceResource)
 53→        initSubscreen()
 54→    }
 55→
 56→    abstract fun initSubscreen()
 57→
 58→    override fun onPreferenceTreeClick(preference: Preference): Boolean {
 59→        UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsEvent(
 60→            category = UsageAnalytics.Category.SETTING,
 61→            action = UsageAnalytics.Actions.TAPPED_SETTING,
 62→            label = preference.key
 63→        )
 64→        return super.onPreferenceTreeClick(preference)
 65→    }
 66→
 67→    override fun onSharedPreferenceChanged(sharedPreferences: SharedPreferences, key: String?) {
 68→        if (key !in UsageAnalytics.preferencesWhoseChangesShouldBeReported) {
 69→            return
 70→        }
 71→        if (key != null) {
 72→            val valueToReport = getPreferenceReportableValue(sharedPreferences.get(key))
 73→            UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsEvent(
 74→                category = UsageAnalytics.Category.SETTING,
 75→                action = UsageAnalytics.Actions.CHANGED_SETTING,
 76→                value = valueToReport,
 77→                label = key
 78→            )
 79→        }
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    override fun onCreatePreferences(savedInstanceState: Bundle?, rootKey: String?) {
 83→        UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsScreenView(analyticsScreenNameConstant)
 84→        addPreferencesFromResource(preferenceResource)
 85→        initSubscreen()
 86→    }
 87→
 88→    protected abstract val analyticsScreenNameConstant: String
 89→
 90→    @Suppress("deprecation") // setTargetFragment #9452
 91→    // androidx.preference.PreferenceDialogFragmentCompat uses the deprecated method
 92→    // `getTargetFragment()`, which throws if `setTargetFragment()` isn't used before.
 93→    // While this isn't fixed on upstream, suppress the deprecation warning
 94→    override fun onDisplayPreferenceDialog(preference: Preference) {
 95→        val dialogFragment = (preference as? DialogFragmentProvider)?.makeDialogFragment()
 96→            ?: return super.onDisplayPreferenceDialog(preference)
 97→        Timber.d("displaying custom preference: ${dialogFragment::class.simpleName}")
 98→        dialogFragment.arguments = bundleOf("key" to preference.key)
 99→        dialogFragment.setTargetFragment(this, 0)
100→        dialogFragment.show(parentFragmentManager, "androidx.preference.PreferenceFragment.DIALOG")
101→    }
102→
103→    override fun onStart() {
104→        super.onStart()
105→        requireActivity().title = preferenceScreen.title
106→        PreferenceManager.getDefaultSharedPreferences(requireContext())
107→            .registerOnSharedPreferenceChangeListener(this)
108→    }
109→
110→    override fun onStop() {
111→        super.onStop()
112→        PreferenceManager.getDefaultSharedPreferences(requireContext())
113→            .unregisterOnSharedPreferenceChangeListener(this)
114→    }
115→
116→    protected fun allPreferences(): List<Preference> {
117→        val allPreferences = mutableListOf<Preference>()
118→        for (i in 0 until preferenceScreen.preferenceCount) {
119→            val pref = preferenceScreen.getPreference(i)
120→            if (pref is PreferenceCategory) {
121→                for (j in 0 until pref.preferenceCount) {
122→                    allPreferences.add(pref.getPreference(j))
123→                }
124→            } else {
125→                allPreferences.add(pref)
126→            }
127→        }
128→        return allPreferences
129→    }
130→
131→    companion object {
132→        @JvmStatic // Using protected members which are not @JvmStatic in the superclass companion is unsupported yet
133→        protected fun getSubscreenIntent(context: Context, fragmentClass: KClass<out SettingsFragment>): Intent {
134→            return Intent(context, Preferences::class.java)
135→                .putExtra(Preferences.INITIAL_FRAGMENT_EXTRA, fragmentClass.jvmName)
136→        }
137→
138→        /**
139→         * Converts a preference value to a numeric number that
140→         * can be reported to analytics, since analytics events only accept
141→         * [Int] as value ([UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsEvent]),
142→         * or null if it can't be converted.
143→         *
144→         * Boolean preferences will return 1 if true and 0 if false
145→         *
146→         * String preferences whose values are stored in a numeric format,
147→         * e.g. fullscreen mode whose values are "0", "1" and "2",
148→         * can have their values reported as well.
149→         * */
150→        @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.PRIVATE)
151→        fun getPreferenceReportableValue(value: Any?): Int? {
152→            return when (value) {
153→                is Int -> value
154→                is String -> try { value.toInt() } catch (e: NumberFormatException) { null }
155→                is Boolean -> if (value) 1 else 0
156→                is Float -> value.toInt()
157→                is Long -> value.toInt()
158→                else -> null
159→            }
160→        }
161→    }
162→}

 18→package com.ichi2.preferences
 19→
 20→import android.content.Context
 21→import android.util.AttributeSet
 22→import android.view.Gravity
 23→import android.view.View
 24→import android.view.ViewGroup
 25→import android.widget.Button
 26→import android.widget.EditText
 27→import android.widget.LinearLayout
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 29→
 30→/** Marker class to be used in preferences */
 31→class IncrementerNumberRangePreferenceCompat : NumberRangePreferenceCompat, DialogFragmentProvider {
 32→    @Suppress("unused")
 33→    constructor(context: Context, attrs: AttributeSet?, defStyleAttr: Int, defStyleRes: Int) : super(context, attrs, defStyleAttr, defStyleRes)
 34→
 35→    @Suppress("unused")
 36→    constructor(context: Context, attrs: AttributeSet?, defStyleAttr: Int) : super(context, attrs, defStyleAttr)
 37→
 38→    @Suppress("unused")
 39→    constructor(context: Context, attrs: AttributeSet?) : super(context, attrs)
 40→
 41→    @Suppress("unused")
 42→    constructor(context: Context) : super(context)
 43→
 44→    class IncrementerNumberRangeDialogFragmentCompat : NumberRangeDialogFragmentCompat() {
 45→        private var lastValidEntry = 0
 46→
 47→        /**
 48→         * Sets [.mEditText] width and gravity.
 49→         */
 50→        override fun onBindDialogView(view: View) {
 51→            super.onBindDialogView(view)
 52→
 53→            // Layout parameters for mEditText
 54→            val editTextParams = LinearLayout.LayoutParams(
 55→                LinearLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
 56→                LinearLayout.LayoutParams.WRAP_CONTENT,
 57→                3.0f
 58→            )
 59→
 60→            lastValidEntry = try {
 61→                editText.text.toString().toInt()
 62→            } catch (nfe: NumberFormatException) {
 63→                // This should not be possible but just in case, recover with a valid minimum from superclass
 64→                numberRangePreference.min
 65→            }
 66→            editText.layoutParams = editTextParams
 67→            // Centre text inside mEditText
 68→            editText.gravity = Gravity.CENTER_HORIZONTAL
 69→        }
 70→
 71→        /**
 72→         * Sets appropriate Text and OnClickListener for buttons
 73→         *
 74→         * Sets orientation for layout
 75→         */
 76→        override fun onCreateDialogView(context: Context): View {
 77→            val linearLayout = LinearLayout(context)
 78→
 79→            val incrementButton = Button(context)
 80→            val decrementButton = Button(context)
 81→            val dialogView = super.onCreateDialogView(context)!!
 82→            val editText: EditText = dialogView.findViewById(android.R.id.edit)
 83→            (editText.parent as ViewGroup).removeView(editText)
 84→
 85→            // Layout parameters for incrementButton and decrementButton
 86→            val buttonParams = LinearLayout.LayoutParams(
 87→                LinearLayout.LayoutParams.WRAP_CONTENT,
 88→                LinearLayout.LayoutParams.WRAP_CONTENT,
 89→                1.0f
 90→            )
 91→
 92→            incrementButton.setText(R.string.plus_sign)
 93→            decrementButton.setText(R.string.minus_sign)
 94→            incrementButton.layoutParams = buttonParams
 95→            decrementButton.layoutParams = buttonParams
 96→            incrementButton.setOnClickListener { updateEditText(true) }
 97→            decrementButton.setOnClickListener { updateEditText(false) }
 98→            linearLayout.orientation = LinearLayout.HORIZONTAL
 99→
100→            linearLayout.addView(decrementButton)
101→            linearLayout.addView(editText)
102→            linearLayout.addView(incrementButton)
103→            return linearLayout
104→        }
105→
106→        /**
107→         * Increments/Decrements the value of [.mEditText] by 1 based on the parameter value.
108→         *
109→         * @param isIncrement Indicator for whether to increase or decrease the value.
110→         */
111→        private fun updateEditText(isIncrement: Boolean) {
112→            var value: Int = try {
113→                editText.text.toString().toInt()
114→            } catch (e: NumberFormatException) {
115→                // If the user entered a non-number then incremented, restore to a good value
116→                lastValidEntry
117→            }
118→            value = if (isIncrement) value + 1 else value - 1
119→
120→            // Make sure value is within range
121→            lastValidEntry = numberRangePreference.getValidatedRangeFromInt(value)
122→            editText.setText(lastValidEntry.toString())
123→            editText.setSelection(editText.text.length)
124→        }
125→    }
126→
127→    override fun makeDialogFragment() = IncrementerNumberRangeDialogFragmentCompat()
128→}

 16→package com.ichi2.preferences
 17→
 18→import android.content.Context
 19→import android.text.InputFilter.LengthFilter
 20→import android.text.InputType
 21→import android.util.AttributeSet
 22→import android.view.View
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidApp
 24→import timber.log.Timber
 25→
 26→@Suppress("deprecation", "OVERRIDE_DEPRECATION") // TODO Tracked in https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/issues/5019 : use NumberRangePreferenceCompat
 27→open class NumberRangePreference : android.preference.EditTextPreference, AutoFocusable {
 28→    protected val min: Int
 29→    private val max: Int
 30→
 31→    constructor(context: Context?, attrs: AttributeSet?, defStyle: Int) : super(context, attrs, defStyle) {
 32→        min = getMinFromAttributes(attrs)
 33→        max = getMaxFromAttributes(attrs)
 34→        updateSettings()
 35→    }
 36→
 37→    constructor(context: Context?, attrs: AttributeSet?) : super(context, attrs) {
 38→        min = getMinFromAttributes(attrs)
 39→        max = getMaxFromAttributes(attrs)
 40→        updateSettings()
 41→    }
 42→
 43→    constructor(context: Context?) : super(context) {
 44→        min = getMinFromAttributes(null)
 45→        max = getMaxFromAttributes(null)
 46→        updateSettings()
 47→    }
 48→
 49→    override fun onBindDialogView(view: View?) {
 50→        super.onBindDialogView(view)
 51→        autoFocusAndMoveCursorToEnd(editText)
 52→    }
 53→
 54→    override fun onDialogClosed(positiveResult: Boolean) {
 55→        if (positiveResult) {
 56→            val validated = getValidatedRangeFromString(editText.text.toString())
 57→            value = validated
 58→        }
 59→    }
 60→
 61→    /*
 62→     * Since this preference deals with integers only, it makes sense to only store and retrieve integers. However,
 63→     * since it is extending EditTextPreference, the persistence and retrieval methods that are called are for a String
 64→     * type. The two methods below intercept the persistence and retrieval methods for Strings and replaces them with
 65→     * their Integer equivalents.
 66→     */
 67→    override fun getPersistedString(defaultReturnValue: String?): String? {
 68→        return getPersistedInt(min).toString()
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    override fun persistString(value: String): Boolean {
 72→        return persistInt(value.toInt())
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    /**
 76→     * Return the string as an int with the number rounded to the nearest bound if it is outside of the acceptable
 77→     * range.
 78→     *
 79→     * @param input User input in text editor.
 80→     * @return The input value within acceptable range.
 81→     */
 82→    private fun getValidatedRangeFromString(input: String): Int {
 83→        return if (input.isEmpty()) {
 84→            min
 85→        } else {
 86→            try {
 87→                getValidatedRangeFromInt(input.toInt())
 88→            } catch (e: NumberFormatException) {
 89→                Timber.w(e)
 90→                min
 91→            }
 92→        }
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    /**
 96→     * Return the integer rounded to the nearest bound if it is outside of the acceptable range.
 97→     *
 98→     * @param input Integer to validate.
 99→     * @return The input value within acceptable range.
100→     */
101→    protected fun getValidatedRangeFromInt(input: Int): Int {
102→        var result = input
103→        if (input < min) {
104→            result = min
105→        } else if (input > max) {
106→            result = max
107→        }
108→        return result
109→    }
110→
111→    /**
112→     * Returns the value of the min attribute, or its default value if not specified
113→     *
114→     *
115→     * This method should only be called once from the constructor.
116→     */
117→    private fun getMinFromAttributes(attrs: AttributeSet?): Int {
118→        return attrs?.getAttributeIntValue(AnkiDroidApp.XML_CUSTOM_NAMESPACE, "min", 0) ?: 0
119→    }
120→
121→    /**
122→     * Returns the value of the max attribute, or its default value if not specified
123→     *
124→     *
125→     * This method should only be called once from the constructor.
126→     */
127→    private fun getMaxFromAttributes(attrs: AttributeSet?): Int {
128→        return attrs?.getAttributeIntValue(AnkiDroidApp.XML_CUSTOM_NAMESPACE, "max", Int.MAX_VALUE)
129→            ?: Int.MAX_VALUE
130→    }
131→
132→    /**
133→     * Update settings to only allow integer input and set the maximum number of digits allowed in the text field based
134→     * on the current value of the [.mMax] field.
135→     *
136→     *
137→     * This method should only be called once from the constructor.
138→     */
139→    private fun updateSettings() {
140→        // Only allow integer input
141→        editText.inputType = InputType.TYPE_CLASS_NUMBER
142→
143→        // Set max number of digits
144→        editText.filters += LengthFilter(max.toString().length)
145→    }
146→    var value: Int
147→        /**
148→         * Get the persisted value held by this preference.
149→         *
150→         * @return the persisted value.
151→         */
152→        get() = getPersistedInt(min)
153→
154→        /**
155→         * Set this preference's value. The value is validated and persisted as an Integer.
156→         *
157→         * @param value to set.
158→         */
159→        set(value) {
160→            val validated = getValidatedRangeFromInt(value)
161→            text = validated.toString()
162→            persistInt(validated)
163→        }
164→}
